site_description: A community transparency portal for General Trias City, Cavite — find government services, officials, budgets, and public information.

hero.title: BetterGenTri
hero.subtitle: Access government services, information, and resources for the people of General Trias City, Cavite.

services.title: Services & Information
services.description: Access official General Trias City government services quickly and easily. Find what you need for citizenship, business, health, education, and more.

health-services.name: Health Services
health-services.description: Where to go for free check-ups, vaccines, and medicines, which hospitals are available, and details on health programs in your area.

education.name: Education
education.description: Where to enroll kids in daycare or preschool, how to apply for scholarships, and what education support your LGU offers.

business.name: Business and Livelihood
business.description: What permits and clearances you need, where to pay taxes or renew permits, and what livelihood or trade opportunities are offered.

social-welfare.name: Social Welfare
social-welfare.description: What assistance is available for seniors, PWDs, solo parents, and indigent families, plus details on relief goods, and livelihood aid.

agriculture-fisheries.name: Agriculture & Fisheries
agriculture-fisheries.description: What farm or fishing support is available, including seeds, fertilizers, veterinary care, and where to join training or borrow equipment.

infrastructure-public-works.name: Infrastructure & Public Works
infrastructure-public-works.description: How to report damaged roads or drainage, where to request water supply, and how to access public facilities and markets.

garbage-waste-disposal.name: Garbage and Waste Disposal
garbage-waste-disposal.description: Garbage collection schedules, waste disposal guidelines, and how to properly dispose of different types of waste.

environment.name: Environment
environment.description: Clean-up or tree planting activities, environmental protection rules, and conservation programs.

disaster-preparedness.name: Disaster Preparedness
disaster-preparedness.description: Disaster preparedness plans, emergency response information, and evacuation procedures.

housing-land-use.name: Housing & Land Use
housing-land-use.description: How to apply for housing projects, what zoning and building permits are needed, and where relocation sites are available.

tourism.name: Tourism
tourism.description: Tourist spots, permits for tourism-related businesses, accreditation, and visitor information for General Trias City.

tourismSpotlight.title: Discover General Trias City
tourismSpotlight.description: General Trias is one of the fastest-growing cities in the Philippines, home to historical churches, lush parks, the Eagle Ridge Golf Club, and the vibrant Valenciana Festival.

governmentActivity.title: City Government
governmentActivity.description: Stay updated with the latest news, departments, and activities of the General Trias City Government.

departments.name: Departments
departments.description: Explore local government departments, agencies, and offices with their mandates, services, and contact details.

news.name: News
news.description: Stay informed with the latest local government news, announcements, and policy updates.

guides-and-regulations.name: Guides and Regulations
guides-and-regulations.description: Access guidelines, rules, regulations, and step-by-step instructions for local government processes.

reports-and-statistics.name: Reports and Statistics
reports-and-statistics.description: Access local government reports, statistical data, and performance metrics.

public-consultations.name: Public Consultations
public-consultations.description: Participate in public consultations and provide feedback on local government decisions.

transparency-documents.name: Transparency Documents
transparency-documents.description: Access Freedom of Information releases and transparency documents for local government accountability.

weatherMap.title: Weather and Map of General Trias

history.title: Brief History of General Trias City
history.charteredDesc: General Trias became the 145th city in the Philippines and the 7th in Cavite Province, granted cityhood on December 17, 2015 under R.A. 10675.
history.populationDesc: General Trias is one of the fastest-growing cities in the Philippines, with over 450,000 residents as of the 2020 census, driven by rapid industrialization.

founded.title: Municipal Foundation
founded.text: The area was established as the Municipality of San Francisco de Malabon on December 13, 1748. The name 'Malabon' is believed to have originated from 'Labong,' referring to bamboo shoots found abundantly in the region.

revolution.title: First Cry of Cavite
revolution.text: The 'First Cry of Cavite' occurred in Pasong Kalabaw on August 31, 1896, led by Filipino revolutionaries. General Trias played a crucial role in the Philippine Revolution against Spanish colonial rule.

independence.title: Independence Proclamation
independence.text: The town's Franciscan-built church (est. 1611) was where the Philippine national anthem was first performed during the independence proclamation on June 12, 1898, cementing the city's place in national history.

renamed.title: Renamed to General Trias
renamed.text: Act No. 2889 changed the municipality's name from Malabon to General Trias on February 24, 1920, honoring General Mariano Trias — Cavite's first provincial governor under American administration.

growth.title: Industrial Growth Era
growth.text: General Trias emerged as a major industrial and residential hub in Cavite. Numerous economic zones, manufacturing plants, and subdivisions were established, fueling rapid population growth.

cityhood.title: Cityhood Achieved
cityhood.text: General Trias was officially converted into a component city on December 17, 2015, becoming the 145th city in the Philippines and the 7th city in the province of Cavite under R.A. 10675.

recognition.title: Awards & Recognition
recognition.text: The city earned multiple honors including the Seal of Good Local Governance, Most Business-Friendly LGU Award, and the 2023 Good Financial Housekeeping Passer from DILG-R4A, reflecting strong governance.

footer.mission: Empowering the people of General Trias with transparent access to government services, programs, and public funds.
